title: Pit-Loss Adjusted Optimal Stint Gap
summary: Strategy models compare degradation loss against pit-lane time loss; simple expected tyre life misses race-specific pit loss and degradation slope.
rationale: Strategy models compare degradation loss against pit-lane time loss; simple expected tyre life misses race-specific pit loss and degradation slope. Public F1 modelling uses pit loss and degradation to estimate optimal stint length and undercut timing.
implementation_hint: Infer race-year pit-loss proxy from rows with PitStop=1 using LapTime spikes versus neighboring non-pit laps or race median pace. Estimate degradation slope per compound/race from Cumulative_Degradation/TyreLife or lap-time residual trend. Add features: sqrt(2*pit_loss/degradation_slope), TyreLife minus optimal_stint, and in_optimal_window bands.
expected_effect: Converts raw wear into a race-specific decision boundary, likely useful where the same TyreLife means different pit urgency by circuit.
risk: LapTime outliers can corrupt inferred pit loss; winsorize lap times and use smoothed race/compound fallbacks.
